flashcards.front: What are the four phases of the C++ compilation process?
flashcards.back: Preprocessing (handles directives like #include, #define), Compilation (translates C++ to assembly language), Assembly (converts assembly to object code/machine instructions), and Linking (combines object files and libraries to create the executable).

flashcards.front: What is the role of the preprocessor in C++ compilation?
flashcards.back: The preprocessor processes directives before compilation: it includes header files (#include), expands macros (#define), and performs conditional compilation (#ifdef). It operates on the source code text and produces a modified translation unit for the compiler.

flashcards.front: What is the difference between compilation and assembly phases?
flashcards.back: Compilation translates C++ source code to assembly language (platform-specific). Assembly converts that assembly code into object code (binary .o or .obj files containing machine instructions and relocatable addresses). One C++ file may generate one object file.

flashcards.front: Explain static linking versus dynamic linking in C++.
flashcards.back: Static linking embeds library code into the executable at compile time (larger binary, self-contained). Dynamic linking defers linking to runtime, using shared libraries (.so on Linux, .dll on Windows), resulting in smaller executables and allowing library updates without recompilation.

flashcards.front: Write a g++ command to compile a C++ program with optimization and produce debug information.
flashcards.back: g++ -O2 -g -o output_name source.cpp compiles source.cpp with level 2 optimization (-O2) and debug symbols (-g), producing the executable output_name. Common flags: -Wall (warnings), -std=c++17 (C++ standard), -c (compile only, no linking).

flashcards.front: What is the difference between a compilation error and a linking error?
flashcards.back: Compilation errors (syntax errors, undefined types) occur during the compilation phase and prevent object file generation. Linking errors (undefined reference, multiple definition) occur during linking when symbol references cannot be resolved between object files or libraries.

flashcards.front: What is the purpose of a Makefile in C++ projects?
flashcards.back: A Makefile defines build rules and dependencies, allowing incremental builds (only recompiling changed files). It uses make utility to compare file timestamps and execute commands, automating the compilation pipeline and saving build time in large projects.

flashcards.front: What does the 'undefined reference to' linker error typically indicate?
flashcards.back: This error occurs when a function or variable is declared but not defined (no implementation provided), or when required object files/libraries are not linked. Example: calling a function from another .cpp file without including its object file in the g++ command.
